focus: fix verification, edge-case stress, and concrete test plan

graphEvents.label: All indexFiles callers verified; only scan handler needs explicit skipFreshFiles override
Option A dedupe is acceptable for overloads and lexical collisions under current identity model
Concrete indexFiles option tests drafted for skipFreshFiles true/false/default
Concrete scan handler tests drafted for fullScanRequested and effectiveIncremental
Follow up: separate discovered candidate count from parsed/persisted file counts

summary: Enumerated all indexFiles() call sites and confirmed only code-graph/handlers/scan.ts must pass { skipFreshFiles: effectiveIncremental }. ensure-ready.ts should keep stale-only default because it performs bounded minimum-necessary readiness indexing.

finding: Multiple overload signatures collapse to one (filePath, fqName, kind) symbolId. Dedupe is lossy but consistent with current graph identity; Option B line suffix would make ids edit-position-sensitive.

Decorator names are metadata and do not alter fqName, so repeated @overload definitions collapse. Dedupe is acceptable for the crash fix; follow-up parser work can prefer concrete implementations or preserve overload metadata.

scenario: Anonymous functions / closures with same variable name
finding: Tree-sitter can capture same-name function-valued declarations without lexical parent scope. Dedupe may drop a distinct closure, but the current identity model cannot query both distinctly.
Generated cases are call expressions, not declarations, so they should not create duplicate graph nodes unless repeated helper declarations exist.

summary: Implement IndexFilesOptions.skipFreshFiles defaulting true; call indexFiles(config, { skipFreshFiles: effectiveIncremental }) from scan.ts.
Implement first-seen symbolId dedupe inside capturesToNodes(), seeded with the module node symbolId.
Add fullScanRequested and effectiveIncremental to scan response; keep fullReindexTriggered unchanged.
Line-suffixed symbolId-on-collision preserves more nodes but weakens stable identity and makes symbol IDs sensitive to nearby edits.
Add skipFreshFiles=false, skipFreshFiles=true, and omitted-option tests using temp TypeScript fixtures and mocked isFileStale.
Assert incremental:false passes { skipFreshFiles:false }, exposes fullScanRequested/effectiveIncremental, and remains idempotent across repeated full scans.
Assert duplicate captures produce unique symbolIds, preserve the first logical symbol, and keep exactly one module node.
